projects.name: Zaturn
projects.description: Zaturn provides tools that enable AI models to run SQL, so you don't have to. It can be used as an MCP or as a web interface similar to Jupyter Notebook. It can connect to multiple types of data sources and also make visualizations, all through chat.
projects.short_description: Open source, AI-powered data analysis co-pilot

form.question: How to support
form.response: Use it, give feedback, donate and advocate

form.question: A small brief about your project
form.response: Open Source data analysis with VIBES for everyone.

form.question: One FOSS maintainer lesson for your younger self
form.response: Keep building, keep talking about it.

form.question: Why do you do it? Why do you bother maintaining a FOSS project?
form.response: It's fun and I get to do it the way I'd like to.

form.question: If your repo had a theme song, what would it be?
Which file in your project would you most like to set on fire?
What's your open-source villain origin story?
form.response: It's 2025, and it feels weird to be writing SQL for simple questions about my data.

form.question: If you had to use one emoji to convey what it's like to be a FOSS maintainer, what would it be?
form.response: 💀 (I think my least favorite parts of being a FOSS maintainer are dealing with emojis and Discord)
